title: UIAO Tier-2 Contract Fixture Schema
description: JSON Schema for tier-2 contract fixtures stored under tests/fixtures/contract/<adapter>/. Each fixture encodes one expected vendor request/response pair plus the adapter behavior contract. The cross-cutting filesystem invariant (adapter field must match the parent directory name) is enforced by tests/contract/test_fixture_schema.py since it cannot be expressed in JSON Schema. Per UIAO_131 §3.2 and tests/fixtures/contract/README.md.

adapter.description: Kebab-case adapter identifier. Must match the parent directory name (filesystem invariant enforced by the Python test runner).

operation.description: Operation name in snake_case; identifies the adapter method this fixture exercises.

cloud_variant.description: Cloud variants the fixture applies to. Optional; absence means variant-agnostic. Catches typos like 'gcc' instead of 'gcc-moderate'.

headers.description: Request headers. Bearer tokens / API keys redacted to <TOKEN> / <API_KEY> per the contract README's sanitization rules.

body.description: Request body; null for GETs. Sanitized per the contract README §sanitization rules.

status.description: HTTP status code returned by the vendor for this request.

body.description: Response body. Sanitized per the contract README §sanitization rules.

expected_behavior.description: List of SHALL-statements the adapter must satisfy when handling this request/response pair. Each item is testable and concrete (not aspirational).

source.description: One of the documented source classes OR a 'finding-<id>' identifier (e.g. 'finding-001'). Source class drives sanitization expectations — see contract README §Source classes.

captured_by.description: Human name OR adapter-test-run-id. Identifies who or what produced this fixture so the audit chain is intact.

sanitization.description: Free-text declaration of what was redacted, replaced, or omitted before commit. Authoritative for the human reviewer.

url.description: Top-level provenance URL when the fixture cites a single public source (alternative to primary_citation.url for simple cases).

accessed.description: ISO 8601 date the URL was accessed. Required when 'url' is set; the contract README treats undated citations as drift-prone.

id.description: Stable identifier of the governance finding this fixture codifies (e.g. 'FINDING-001').

path.description: Repo-relative path to the canonical finding document.

quote.description: Verbatim quote from the cited source supporting the fixture's contract.
